target.title: fix(telegram): keep streamed tool-progress lines on separate lines
target.body:
Small formatting fix: in the new streaming renderer a newline became a separation character, so streamed tool-progress lines ran together on one line instead of stacking. cc @peetiegonzalez @obviyus

### Summary

Telegram's rich-markdown renderer treats a lone `\n` as a soft break (it renders as a space). The streamed progress draft joins its lines with a single `\n`, which worked while the draft was sent as native entities. Since the native-entity draft preview was removed and the draft now goes through the rich-markdown path, those single newlines collapse and the 🛠️ tool lines (and 🧠 / 💬 lines) mash onto one line.

Fix: add a generic `lineSeparator` option to the shared progress-draft formatter (default `\n`) and have the Telegram channel pass `\n\n`. Telegram renders a blank line as a *single* visual break, so each line gets its own line again. Every other channel keeps the single-newline default, so Discord/Slack/Matrix/MSTeams are unaffected.

Three lines changed in prod (one option threaded through two functions, one call site), plus four Telegram draft-text test assertions updated to the corrected output.

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** streamed Telegram tool-progress lines collapsing onto one line (single `\n` soft-break) instead of stacking vertically.
- **Real environment tested:** live Telegram bot via `sendRichMessage` (Bot API rich markdown), the same transport the channel uses.
- **Exact steps/command run after this patch:** sent the exact fixed progress-draft string (`Working\n\n🛠️ …\n\n🛠️ …\n\n🛠️ …`) the compositor now produces; also A/B'd single-`\n` vs `\n\n` directly against the server.
- **Evidence after fix:** single-`\n` variant renders on one line; `\n\n` variant renders three separate lines (and as a single break, not a double).
- **Observed result after fix:**
  ```
  Working
  🛠️ run sleep 8 && date
  🛠️ ls workspace
  🛠️ wc -l largest two
  ```
- **What was not tested:** non-Telegram channels (unchanged by default), and the full local suite (ran the affected unit tests + typecheck only).

### Verification

- `progress-draft-compositor` unit tests: 11/11 pass
- `bot-message-dispatch` Telegram tests: 118/118 pass (4 draft-text assertions updated to the corrected `\n\n` join)
- `tsgo` core + extensions: clean
- `oxfmt --check` on changed files: clean
